title: fix: scan tail for oversized safety input
author: voodoohop

gist.headline: The 50k safety net
gist.blurb: We've updated our safety filters to handle massive text prompts without breaking a sweat. Instead of rejecting oversized inputs, we now scan the tail end of your prompt to keep things running smoothly.
gist.summary: Updated the Bedrock Guardrails safety middleware to scan the last 50,000 characters of oversized inputs instead of failing the request.
gist.impact: Users can now send massive text prompts without triggering safety-related input size errors.
gist.visual_concept: Nomnom creature eating a massive, endlessly long paper scroll (representing the 50k text input). Polly the bee is inspecting the very end (the tail) of the scroll with a magnifying glass (safety check).
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the round tan Nomnom creature happily eating a massive, endlessly long paper scroll of text. The cute bee mascot hovers near the very end of the scroll, inspecting the tail end with a tiny magnifying glass. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Chunky pixels, warm lighting, lo-fi aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Emotionally warm, nostalgic but beautiful.

image.prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the round tan Nomnom creature happily eating a massive, endlessly long paper scroll of text.
